services.permanence_description: We accompany scholarship programs, bootcamps, and universities through a comprehensive intervention process developed with specialized approaches to enhance students’ progress and well-being.
services.persistence_consulting_description: From which the Foundation is consolidated as a strategic ally for the implementation of actions focused on ensuring educational persistence.
services.socio_vocational_guidance_description: Aware of the importance of the life project in young people, the foundation strengthens conscious and informed decision-making through self-knowledge, and approaching the educational and labor worlds.
services.atvconnect_description: A personalized and innovative service that combines academic support, technology, and comprehensive well-being to close learning gaps and strengthen student retention.

donation.description: At the Fundación Antivirus para la Deserción, we are a non-profit organization that seeks to reduce student dropout levels in Colombia, improve retention and graduation rates, this being our way of contributing to achieving a country with more education and consequently more development.
donation.cop: All donations are processed in Colombian Pesos
donation.monthly_payment: Allow the payment to be monthly according to the mentioned amount

banner.title: what we do
banner.alt_banner: Banner with the title 'what we do'

contentDA.title: Our Model
contentDA.subtitle: Data Analytics
contentDA.paragraph1: The Data Analytics area is based on a comprehensive and open-source solution for student retention supported by technology, where Machine Learning and Big Data are used to respond to early warnings of student dropout risk and analyze the root causes that produce it.
contentDA.paragraph2: In addition, through a web application, intervention cases are managed to act on the data and support is provided for proper adoption from the wellness area with expert consultations on retention.

commun.subtitle: COMMUNICATIONS
commun.paragraph_1: This area is made up of social communicators and graphic designers.
commun.paragraph_2: This team creates and promotes communication actions, adjusted to the characterization and built through the needs and segmentation of the students.
commun.paragraph_3: This area is responsible for disseminating information and creating audiovisual content and using technological means, platforms, and other interaction and information resources.
commun.paragraph_4: The Communication and Marketing area is also in charge of digital marketing and email marketing to the project's stakeholders, such as directors, teachers, students, and companies interested in the services provided by FATV.

change_theory.title: THEORY OF CHANGE
change_theory.description: Identify students with vulnerabilities and provide comprehensive support through an ecosystem of strategies focused on academic, economic and socio-emotional aspects for retention.

values.title: VALUES
values.description: At the Antivirus Foundation for Dropout Prevention, we do our work collaboratively, with passion, integrity, autonomy, empathy, commitment and camaraderie. We are supportive and innovative and have a great vocation for service.

mission.title: MISSION
mission.description: Increase graduation rates of students in technology careers and programs, through strategies to promote retention and prevent dropout.

target_audience.title: TARGET AUDIENCE
target_audience.description: We work with students from IT careers and programs in Secondary and Post-secondary Education. We focus our intervention on students vulnerable to dropout.

vision.title: VISION
vision.description: In 5 years we will have a national presence, in the 10 best universities in the country, where we will have reduced the dropout rate by 10 percentage points (from 50 to 40%). Additionally, we will be replicating a reference model, whose success can be measured qualitatively and quantitatively.

items.consolidate_model: Consolidate a model with processes for identifying vulnerabilities, intervention and result indicators.
items.explore_interventions: Explore intervention models from schools to contribute to reducing dropout in higher education.
items.involve_actors: Know and involve other actors in retention work (Rectors, Deans, MEN, Companies, among others).
items.reduce_dropout: Help reduce dropout in other universities and programs to refine our model and generate income that contributes to the sustainability of the Antivirus Foundation.

purpose.title: PURPOSE
purpose.description: Build a prosperous, peaceful, harmonious, technological and competitive country, in which all young people have access to education and are good human beings, with goals and empowered in their lives. Thanks to this, we will overcome poverty and become a reference for development.

0.alt: 2024: A year of movement, challenges and transformation
0.title: 2024: A YEAR OF MOVEMENT, CHALLENGES AND TRANSFORMATION

detail.title: 2024: A YEAR OF MOVEMENT, CHALLENGES AND TRANSFORMATION
content: 2024 was a year of challenges, questions, movement and management for the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation, a year that invited us to rethink the path and the ways in which, as a social organization, we contribute to society. In the midst of a challenging environment, we reaffirmed our commitment to education and the support of thousands of young people in the country.

The management report reflects the perseverance, love, innovation and passion that moved each of our actions to fulfill our missionary purpose. We mobilized conversations, campaigns and alliances, making visible the needs of young people not to drop out, we invited students, tutors, teachers, institutions and allies to be part of a network that embraces, listens and walks with them.

During this period, we reflected on the problem, but, above all, we took action, managing permanence at the University of Antioquia and the National University in the Systems Engineering degree. We accompanied the students who were granted scholarships from the Aurelio Llano Posada Foundation, the Nutresa Foundation, the philanthropic platform Ver+ Beca Tech and the municipality of Itagüí; with which we also had a socio-vocational guidance process with 10th grade students in 12 of their public educational institutions. We managed the permanence in the EAFIT Node and C13 Code Bootcamps. Finally, we accompanied and empowered the Liniera students in the training process of the Tener Futuro Corporation.

This report is a reflection of the path traveled, the support provided and the path we have left; it is a reliable proof that when efforts are added, a country is built and stories are changed. Every student we manage to accompany and every young person who graduates is a promise fulfilled. Just as every partner who joins, is part of a more just, close and sustainable education. In 2024, we did not talk only about dropout, we talked about permanence, hope, future and social transformation.

We thank all our partners, who directly or indirectly supported our work to build a more prosperous, peaceful and harmonious country, where dreams matter and triumphs are worked for. To the students who believe in our accompaniment and allow us to walk the path together. To our collaborators who day by day make a change possible and give their five percent for a more conscious society; thank you for believing in the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation and for believing that together we can achieve it.
We will continue to act, with the conviction that we can continue to build support networks that make the dreams of many young people possible.
Read it here: AnnualReport2024_final

1.alt: Renewal of the special regime for transparency
1.title: RENEWAL OF THE SPECIAL REGIME FOR TRANSPARENCY

detail.title: RENEWAL OF THE SPECIAL REGIME FOR TRANSPARENCY
content: The timely submission of the request for updating the recognition as ESAL to enjoy the exemption from income tax before the DIAN was verified in accordance with the requirements; leaving the publication on the Foundation's website pending.

detail.title: UNIVERSITY DROPOUT
content: I have a vision for my country: a prosperous, peaceful, harmonious, technological and competitive place, where everyone has access to education and is a good human being with purpose and empowerment. To achieve this, I focus on addressing the shortage of talent in software development and the high university dropout rate, particularly at the Faculty of Engineering of the University of Antioquia.

The private sector has experienced a deficit of software engineers and programmers, while universities do not meet the demand. We currently graduate only around 7,000 engineers/programmers per year, which leaves us with a talent deficit of between 90,000 and 240,000 professionals. This moves us away from development and the knowledge economy.

University dropout, especially in Systems Engineering, is a national problem. The University of Antioquia faces a high dropout rate, which has a devastating social cost. I propose a mentoring program where advanced students help first-semester students, which has been shown to reduce dropout by 20%. In addition, a system can be established in which companies financially support students in exchange for a percentage of their salaries once they graduate.

It is also important to address students' economic difficulties and family pressure. Here, the private sector can contribute through computer donations, support in structuring financing instruments and the participation of their collaborators in volunteer work.

In short, a joint approach is needed to achieve a shared vision of development in the country. With mentoring programs, financial support from companies and the active participation of the private sector, we can reduce the shortage of talent and university dropout, paving the way towards a prosperous and technological future.

detail.title: COLLOQUIUM AT ENVIADO UNIVERSITY
content: The Antivirus for Desertion Foundation was invited to the 1st Meeting of Industrial and Related Engineering Programs: Challenges and trends.

The director of our Foundation, Luis Fernando Sánchez, was on October 14 participating in this teacher training event held by the REDIN and ACOFI Network, where they talked about teaching practice as a factor associated with permanence or dropout and about early warnings to address the mental health of engineers in training.

4.alt: Participation in the XI Latin American congress on dropout in higher education
4.title: PARTICIPATION IN THE XI LATIN AMERICAN CONGRESS ON DROPOUT IN HIGHER EDUCATION

detail.title: PARTICIPATION IN THE XI LATIN AMERICAN CONGRESS ON DROPOUT IN HIGHER EDUCATION
content: The article "Antivirus for Desertion Foundation: From the psychopedagogical to the sociopedagogical" written and presented by collaborators Víctor Valencia, Jhisel Holguín and Gabriel Castellanos was the successful bet they developed for the presentation at the XI Latin American Congress on Dropout in Higher Education, held in Brasília, on November 16, 2022. This represented an achievement, since it allowed to chart new horizons, present the intervention model currently developed in the projects and share experiences with attendees from several countries in Latin America.

5.alt: Presentation at the international communications congress
5.title: PRESENTATION AT THE INTERNATIONAL COMMUNICATIONS CONGRESS

detail.title: PRESENTATION AT THE INTERNATIONAL COMMUNICATIONS CONGRESS
content: Last November 2, the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation was invited to the International Communications Congress at the University of Medellín, where Daniela Correa, communications coordinator, was in charge of the presentation of 'Communication in social enterprises'. This represented an achievement for the Foundation and its collaborators, as it was an opportunity to publicize the transversal model of communications in a social project and, in turn, promote the purpose of increasing permanence rates.

detail.title: PRESENTATION AT THE INTERNATIONAL COMMUNICATIONS CONGRESS
content: The first version of MVS was experienced simultaneously with TechFest 2022, bringing together experts, businessmen, teachers, students and citizens interested in technology. This space full of workshops, interactive experiences and entertainment was carried out by the Mayor's Office of Medellín.

The Antivirus for Desertion Foundation had the opportunity to participate during the four days of the festival, publicizing the problem of dropout that stands as an educational pandemic in STEM careers; at the same time as its intervention model, encouraging more young people to study these areas.

detail.title: WE RECEIVED AN HONORABLE MENTION
content: The Germán Saldarriaga del Valle 2022 award, given each year by the Rotary Club of Medellín, was in charge of rewarding non-profit institutions whose contribution had provided a solution to the difficulties and educational gaps of the community in times of the Covid-19 pandemic, adapting and innovating within this field. Of the 102 proposals presented from 14 departments of the country, five were the winners of the award and three received an honorable mention, among them the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation.

detail.title: MEETING WITH FATV COLLABORATORS
content: Inspiration in every look! Meet our talented team of collaborators, who with passion and experience lead our Foundation towards the achievement of its objectives. Together, we will face the challenges of 2023!

detail.title: SHARED VISION
content: The power of a shared vision is priceless! Thanks to our friends from Vélez Reyes Más for walking with us in the search for better alternatives to make Colombia a country with quality education, with lower dropout rates, better levels of permanence and graduation.

detail.title: STATE OF EDUCATION IN ANTIOQUIA
content: At ProAntioquia we have contributed with our participation about the state of education in Antioquia. Thematic Meeting Education - Platform for Youth and Businessmen. Proantioquia and the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation united in favor of the quality of Education.

detail.title: OUR INTERIOR DESIGN!
content: The Antivirus for Desertion Foundation and its communications team join forces to transform the interior design of the office in the municipality of La Estrella. Both organizations collaborated closely to create an inspiring and functional environment that promotes creativity and teamwork.

In Communications, the talent and knowledge of designers and communicators were contributed to create a welcoming and stimulating space. They focused on using vibrant colors and familiar designs, as well as the use of Javi, with the aim of improving the productivity and well-being of collaborators at FATV.

The result is a modern and functional office, where the members of the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation can work in an inspiring and collaborative environment. The new interior design reflects the mission and values of the organization, creating a space in line with its commitment to provide education and development opportunities to young people at risk of dropping out.

We are all excited about the result of the construction and installation of the internal visual of the office and we hope that the new interior design of the office in La Estrella will inspire collaborators and further strengthen their work in the community. The Antivirus for Desertion Foundation team celebrates the success of achieving this objective and we are excited about future joint work on projects that promote the well-being and development of the community!

detail.title: OUR MARKET RESEARCH
content: The Antivirus for Desertion Foundation is committed to its mission of providing educational opportunities to young people at risk. In that sense, the Communications team has started an exciting market research to improve its communication processes and effectively achieve the organization's mission, vision and purpose.

The main objective of this research is to better understand the needs and preferences of the different audiences with which the foundation is related, both internal and external. Through data analysis and contextualization of the environment, the Communications team seeks to obtain valuable information that allows them to develop more effective and personalized communication strategies.

This initiative demonstrates the commitment of the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation to adapt to the changing needs of its environment and optimize its communication channels. By fully understanding its audience, the organization will be able to transmit its message clearly and relevantly, generating a positive impact on the community and bringing more young people closer to educational opportunities.

The market research process involves the active participation of the entire Communications team, who work hard to collect, analyze and apply the data obtained. It is expected that the results of this research will allow the identification of new communication strategies, as well as the strengthening of existing ones, to achieve greater effectiveness in the dissemination of the foundation's work and programs.

The Antivirus for Desertion Foundation is excited about the progress of this market research and the positive impact it will have on its work. Through more effective communication, the organization seeks to reach a greater number of young people at risk and provide them with the opportunities they deserve for a better future.

The Communications team of the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation continues to work hard to carry out this research and is eager to implement the improvements that will arise from it. Together, we will achieve more impactful communication and bring our mission even closer to those who need it most!

13.alt: Taking education to new horizons in Colombia
13.title: TAKING EDUCATION TO NEW HORIZONS IN COLOMBIA

detail.title: TAKING EDUCATION TO NEW HORIZONS IN COLOMBIA
content: In a country where higher education is often an elusive dream for many people, the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation embarks on a brave and exciting project in collaboration with the Aurelio Llano Posada Foundation. Its mission is clear and powerful: to reduce student dropout in Colombia, where the figures show a challenge of 50%.

This project seeks not only to change the reality of dropout, but also to improve the quality of educational permanence and maintain graduation indicators that inspire others. The focus? Disruptive strategies that range from psychosocial support to tools of the fourth industrial revolution.

More than 500 peasant students, beneficiaries of the scholarships granted by the Aurelio Llano Posada Foundation, are directly impacted by this effort. These students are distributed in more than 27 higher education institutions throughout Colombia.

The project is deployed in two equally powerful lines of support. The first, Socio-pedagogical Support, is guided by social workers who immerse themselves in the realities of these students. Together, they identify obstacles and trace support routes, connecting students with University Well-being services and other program strategies. Socio-pedagogical advisories offer spaces for listening and conversation, creating bridges to address vulnerabilities and challenges.

The second line, Academic Support, is designed to strengthen students' knowledge in critical areas. Academic mentoring, individual academic review sessions, are led by students with an outstanding academic history, known as mentors. These mentors provide advice on specific topics that often present difficulties.

The "Level up with Antivirus" Workshops are collective spaces where knowledge in various subjects is strengthened. These workshops are designed to ensure that students not only understand the contents, but also pass their subjects. They are led by students with solid academic performance and a clear mastery of the subjects.

"Review Online" is an initiative that offers virtual workshops created by the Antivirus for Desertion Foundation. These workshops focus on subjects with high rates of repetition and cancellations. The videos are shared on the YouTube platform, which makes them a valuable and accessible resource for all students of the Aurelio Llano Posada Foundation.

The expected impact is profound and significant: the reduction of dropout levels and a palpable improvement in the quality of permanence of these brave young people. This project is a testimony to the unwavering commitment of these foundations to higher education in Colombia. In a country where higher education is still a beacon of hope for many, these two foundations work tirelessly to transform those dreams into a bright and achievable reality for all.

A call to action echoes in every step of this project, inviting the community and potential collaborators to join this noble cause. The end of this story is the beginning of a journey, one that promises a brighter and more accessible educational future in Colombia.

14.alt: The importance of data to end dropout
14.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF DATA TO END DROPOUT

detail.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF DATA TO END DROPOUT
content: Data can help enrich stories and can provide valuable information about the things we live every day, providing a source of practical wisdom and giving us guidance on human affairs. We know that talking about algorithms to deal with human lives may seem like a juxtaposition, since for many people the word algorithm evokes arcane and registrable machinery of large companies. But, an algorithm is simply a finite sequence of steps that are used to solve a problem, in which the data provides valuable information to forge each step taken.

There is a recent idea called: “human algorithm design”. This seeks to find better solutions to the challenges that people face every day, whether to track climate change in the world, streamline the search results for an apartment or to end university dropout. While we will delve into the latter in more detail, because this is our purpose, we want to first show how even the most mundane problems can be solved with data. Thus, let's think about the case where Pepita wants to buy an apartment. When this situation was presented to her, Pepita intuited that she required a balance between looking at apartments and choosing the one she wanted. In this way, she foresaw that she should look for enough apartments to establish a standard and, then, commit to the first apartment she sees that exceeds her standard. This means that you need 37% of the time you have to be able to set goals. This type of problem belongs to the class of problems called optimal stopping problems.

The previous example is just an illustration of the capacity of data and statistics. Life is full of problems that are, simply, very difficult. An example of this is the high dropout rate we have in Colombia (50% on average in any faculty and 70% in engineering faculties). This terrifying figure says more about the intrinsic difficulties of this problem than about the fallibility of the human brain. To solve this problem we need to understand its fundamental structure and for this we need data. However, the key value of data is the ability to make the most of it: find relevant associations within the data, manipulate it into manageable sets and then create applications to better represent what is discovered. This was learned by the “Graduation Rate Champion” of the University of Texas, when he was given the challenge of increasing the graduation rate from 51% in 2021 to 70% in 2016.

To achieve his goal, the Graduation Rate Champion recruited a team of data scientists in the university's institutional research office, who were specialized in predictive analytics. By deconstructing the records of tens of thousands of recent students at this University, they were able to develop a model statistic that combined fourteen separate variables. From family income to SAT score, college class rank and parents' educational history, in a single algorithm that could reliably predict the probability that an admitted student would graduate. This team called this new tool the Four-Year Graduation Index Dashboard. (Taugh, 2019) The gain that increasing the number of graduates implies for a university is reflected in a very high social mobility rate. The passage through a university forges the identity formation process of adolescents and really has a positive impact on the way people's lives turn out; how happy they will be and how well they will negotiate the challenges of work, family, friendship and community.

qhfa.intervention_description: This is the foundation's field of action that aims to identify and understand the particularities of the sociocultural and socio-emotional context of students, to influence, transform and significantly impact their conditions and educational process, through tools and alternatives for socio-pedagogical support. The social team is composed of social workers and psychologists responsible for designing and implementing academic strengthening strategies, psycho-emotional and socio-pedagogical support. This area has the following social intervention lines:
qhfa.academic_description: Search for tutors and mentors willing to receive a modest hourly stipend and offer another voluntary hour. In this sense, once tutors are found, this area is responsible for creating appointments for one-on-one tutoring and thematic workshops for complementary class review and study groups.

information.skills_promotion: We promote the development of personal and emotional skills that allow young people to know, value and project themselves.

testimonials_content.description: The support and the people who provide it are very important because you feel confident and feel like you're learning something... The fact that they come with questions that don't force you but encourage you to give an answer has made me better prepared for my future or at least have a clearer plan for what I'm doing today for my future.
testimonials_content.author: - Samuel Durango Baena

testimonials_content.description: We've learned to know ourselves quite well. It has been a personal growth; I've learned to love myself, to know myself more deeply, to look at what I want for my life in a few years, and to know that I have made great progress. I have to be consistent with what I want to achieve and have fixed goals. In the future, I imagine myself as a great professional, having my own company or brand, or being independent.
testimonials_content.author: - Salomé Arango

testimonials_content.description: Honestly, before Antivirus arrived, I was very insecure about what I was going to study or what I was going to do with my life because nobody knows what will happen in the future because it's uncertain. But Antivirus came and little by little has been able to give us security, it has taught us that everything in life has a solution and our problems can become strengths for ourselves.
testimonials_content.author: - Estiwar Espinal

testimonials_content.description: Antivirus has done a wonderful job. Thanks to Antivirus, I've tried to let go of that fear of the future, it has calmed things down a lot. As 11th-grade students, we carry several responsibilities and we worry a lot about what will happen after school. What will I do? And Antivirus has helped us get to know ourselves a little more and know what I can dedicate myself to or which path to take.
testimonials_content.author: - Antonia Aristizábal

testimonials_content.description: The support seemed very pleasant to me; my biggest learning was in decision-making, understanding my emotions better, and thinking before acting. You helped me become more confident in the decisions I want to make in my life. What I liked the most were the talks, the methodology, and the topics; that enlightened us.
testimonials_content.author: - Matías González
